sections.paragraph:
στραγγαλιά, ᾶς, ἡ (Ptolem., Apotel. 4, 9, 10 Boll-B.; Hippiatr. 51, 3; 4, vol. I 228, 9; 229, 7; LXX) knot διαλύειν στραγγαλιὰς βιαίων συναλλαγμάτων untie the knots of forced agreements (i.e. made under duress) B 3:3 (Is 58:6; cp. Plut., Mor. 1033e on philosophers’ knotted arguments).

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

Boll Boll = FB., Aus der Offenb. Johannis—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Hippiatr. Hippiatr. = Corpus hippiatricorum Graecorum, IX a.d.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 947
